title: Clearest Glass
description: No more annoying texture lines on the glass that obstruct the view - only a clean and clear look.
body:
**Clearest Glass** is an elegant, minimalist pack that removes all pixel noise and stripes from the surfaces of glass blocks, making them smooth and clean. Block borders retain their color but gain a crisp black outline for better visual definition. Perfect for modern builds and clean lines.

### Download & Installation
1. Download the file - Click the Files tab above and select the latest version.
2. Place in your resourcepacks folder - In-game, go to Options -> Resource Packs -> Open resource pack folder.
3. Move and activate - Move the downloaded .zip archive into the opened folder, then select texture pack from the list of available packs.
- For the best effect (Optional): Install OptiFine and enable Connected Textures in Video Settings (Quality) or usse Continuity/Fusion mod. This will remove visible seams between blocks, creating the illusion of one large, seamless window or wall.

### Main Features
- Clean Surfaces: All pixelated stripes and noise have been removed from the surfaces of regular glass, tinted glass, and glass panes.
- Crisp Black Borders: Block frames are now a deep black, improving their visibility and definition in structures.
- Stylistic Integrity: Blocks do not become completely invisible but retain their in-game essence with a polished, minimalist look.
- OptiFine Enhanced: For the ultimate seamless experience, use with OptiFine's Connected Textures feature.

### Compatibility
- Minecraft Versions: The pack is updated for all versions from the introduction of tinted glass (1.17) up to the latest stable release (1.20.4 and above).
- Mods: Compatible with mods that add new glass blocks using standard vanilla texture templates.
- Shaders & OptiFine: Recommended for use with OptiFine to access Connected Textures. Fully compatible with major shader packs (BSL, SEUS, Complementary).

### Changelog
Initial release. Removed "noise" from all vanilla glass blocks and panes, set borders on default glass to black.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

A: Yes, under the terms of the GPLv3 license (see above). You must credit the original author (UnderStudioGroup) and provide a link back to this page.
